entries.greek_original: Μοῦσαι ὅππωσ δὴ πρῶτον πῦρ ἔμπεσε

entries.transliteration: Espete nyn moi, Mousai, hopp\=os d\=e pr\=oton pyr empese
naphe kai memnas' apistein: arthra tauta tan phren\=on
hoi men par' ouden eisi, tois d' ouden melei

entries.greek_original: εἷσ οἰωνὸσ ἄριστοσ ἀμύνεσθαι περὶ πάτρης

entries.transliteration: heis oi\=onos aristos amynesthai peri patr\=es

entries.greek_original: φυσᾷ γὰρ οὐ σμικροῖσιν αὐλίσκοισ ἔτι

entries.transliteration: epangellomai andr' apamynesthai hote tis proteros chalep\=en\=ei

entries.greek_original: κἂν ὑπὸ στέγῃ πυκνῆσ ἀκούειν ψακάδοσ εὑδούσῃ φρενί

entries.transliteration: kan hypo steg\=ei pykn\=es akouein psakados heudous\=ei phreni

entries.greek_original: καὶ Κικέρων ὁ μικρὸς ἀσπάζεται Τίτον τὸν Ἀθηναῖον
ἔγωγε ἧσ γαίησ δύναμαι γλυκερώτερον ἄλλο ἰδέσθαι

entries.transliteration: tr\=echei', all' agath\=e kourotrophos, out' ar' eg\=oge h\=es gai\=es dynamai glykerot\=eron allo idesthai
do we keep up the pretence
tortuous and never sound at all
bear with the ways of the powerful
m\=ep\=o meg' eip\=eis prin teleut\=esant' id\=eis

entries.greek_original: σῦκον οὐδὲ ἓν οὕτωσ ὅμοιον γέγονεν

entries.transliteration: suk\=oi, ma t\=en D\=em\=etra, sukon oude hen hout\=os homoion gegonen
ho de mainetai ouk et' anekt\=os
let a man do the work for which he is born
as the mistress, so are the maids
there is a difference between rejoicing and envying
par' emoige kai alloi hoi ke me timesousi, malista de metieta Zeus
let them get nothing from us

entries.greek_original: πεφυρακέναι τὰσ ψήφουσ ἐκ τῆσ ὠνῆσ τῶν ὑπαρχόντων τῶν τοῦ

entries.transliteration: My wife's freedman --- you know the one --- it seemed to me the other day, from what he was letting fall in his calculations, that he has been juggling the accounts in the matter of the purchase of the holdings of\ldots that you may understand. One man, presumably.
and many waves does the south wind roll in the open space of the broad sea

entries.greek_original: τῆσ ξυναόρου τῆσ ἐμῆσ οὑξελεύθεροσ ἔδοξέ

entries.transliteration: my wife's freedman, it seemed to me\ldots

entries.greek_original: ἐξ ἄστεωσ ἑπταλόφου στείχων παρέδωκεν μνῶν κδ

entries.transliteration: Coming from the seven-hilled city, he handed over 24 minae and 48 --- a debt owed to Camillus --- and himself owing 24 minae from the Crotonian holdings and 48 from the Chersonesian, and to have inherited 640, 40. Of these not an obol has been paid out, all of it being owed since the first of the second month. His freedman --- the one who shares a name with the father of Conon --- has given the whole matter not a moment's thought. So then, first that everything may be saved, second that you not pass over the interest from the date already set down: how much we have endured at his hands I am very much afraid. For he was here with us, reconnoitring and hoping for something or other; then, despairing for no good reason, he withdrew, throwing out as he went,
I yield --- shame indeed to linger too long ---,

entries.greek_original: ἐμὸν οὔποτε θυμὸν ἐνὶ στήθεσσιν ἔπειθεσ

entries.transliteration: But you never persuaded the spirit in my breast
the men and women of Troy
a thing not to be regretted
Where is the ship of the Atridae?

entries.greek_original: οὐ γὰρ δὴ τόδε μεῖζον ἔπι κακόν

entries.transliteration: For surely this evil that is on us now is no greater
I have been doing politics with you
that he may have the greatest of the gods, tyranny
take so much leisure for it
regarding what is fitting in itself
I will die with them too

entries.greek_original: πολλὰ μάτην κεράεσσιν ἐσ ἠέρα θυμήναντα

entries.transliteration: tossing his horns idly into the air in his rage

entries.greek_original: ὅ τι χρὴ καὶ παλαμάσθων καὶ πάντ

entries.transliteration: pros tauth' ho ti chrē kai palamasthōn kai pant' ep' emoi tektainesthōn: to gar eu met' emou
autika gar toi epeita meth' Hektora potmos hetoimos
autika tethnaien, epei ouk ar' emellon hetairoi kteinomenoi epamunai
oude moi etor empedon, all' alaluktemai

entries.greek_original: εἰ μενετέον ἐν τῇ πατρίδι τυραννουμένης αὐτῆς
εἰ παντὶ τρόπῳ τυραννίδοσ κατάλυσιν πραγματευτέον

entries.transliteration: ei meneteon en tei patridi turannoumenes autes
ei panti tropoi turannidos katalusin pragmateuteon, kan mellei dia touto peri ton holon he polis kinduneusein
e eulabeteon ton kataluonta me autos airetai
ei peirateon aregein tei patridi turannoumenei kairoi kai logoi mallon e polemoi
ei politikon to hesuchazein anachoresanta poi tes patridos turannoumenes e dia pantos iteon kindunou tes eleutherias peri
ei polemon epakteon tei chorai kai poliorketeon auten turannoumenen
ei kai me dokimazonta ten dia polemou katalusin tes turannidos sunapogrepteon homos tois aristois
ei tois euergetais kai philois sugkinduneuteon en tois politikois kan me dokosin eu bebouleusthai peri ton holon

ei ho megala ten patrida euergetesas di' auto te touto anekesta pathon kai phthonetheis kinduneuseien an etheloutes huper tes patridos e epheteon autoi heautou pote kai ton oikeiotaton poieisthai pronoian aphemenoi tas pros tous ischuontas diapoliteias
hai g\`ar t\^on tur\'ann\=on de\'eseis... oisth' h\'oti memigm\'enai an\'ankais
\'alla men aut\'os \ldots\ \'alla de kai da\'im\=on hupoth\'esetai

entries.greek_original: ἀλλὰ μέγα ῤέξασ τι καὶ ἐσσομένοισι πυθέσθαι

entries.transliteration: m\=e m\'an asp\=oud\'i ge ka\`i akle\=eos, all\`a m\'ega rh\'exas ti ka\`i essomenoisi puth\'esthai

entries.greek_original: τὸ ἄμεινον καὶ τὸ χεῖρον ἐν τῷ ἀφανεῖ ἔτι ἑώρα μάλιστα

entries.transliteration: t\=on men paront\=on di' elachist\=es boul\=es kratistos gn\=om\=on
to ameinon kai to cheiron en t\={o}i aphanei eti he\={o}ra malista
